* GenAI (Generative Adversarial Network) creates synthetic identities that convincingly mimic the device profiles of legitimate users and DER (Distributed Energy Resource) device profiles.

The text describes a research paper on AI-driven attacks on Power Grid Scheduling and Control (PGSC). The attack mechanism is based on a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) that generates control/status messages (x_it) that mimic real messages. The goal is to detect and prevent these fake messages from disrupting the PGSC.

The attack vector is generated using a GAN-based training algorithm (Algorithm 1), which involves training a generator (Gθ) and a discriminator (Dϕ). The generator produces fake messages, and the discriminator evaluates the probability of the fake messages being real.
